research_series.series_name: Brain Fog Phenotypes,Differential Inference,and Mechanism-Specific Measurement

patterns.pattern_name: Subjective brain fog is weakly/inconsistently aligned with objective cognitive impairment

mediator.name: self_report_objective_discordance
mediator.pathway: subjective cognitive complaint and standardized performance partly diverge
mediator.mechanism: symptom experience is influenced by fatigue,mood,sleep,pain,interoception and task sensitivity

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Subjective brain fog should not be automatically treated as objective cognitive impairment; phenotype and context must be specified.

hypotheses.reason: Large cohorts show weak correlations between self-report and objective cognitive tests.

evidence.summary: Subjective brain fog is common but heterogeneous; objective impairments, when present, most often involve attention control and processing speed. Many symptomatic individuals perform normally on standard tests.

key_sources.title: Brain Fog Phenotypes and Differential Characterization: A Structured Review
key_sources.relevant_finding: Subjective brain fog can occur without measurable objective impairment; attention and processing speed are most common objective domains.

limitations: Standard tests may miss subtle acquired impairments

patterns.pattern_name: State-dependent brain fog can be provoked by orthostatic challenge, exertion, migraine phase, sleep deprivation, or drug timing

mediator.name: dynamic_physiological_or_contextual_challenge
mediator.pathway: challenge or phase changes transiently alter cognitive performance and symptom severity
mediator.mechanism: orthostatic cerebral perfusion/autonomic load, post-exertional worsening, migraine postdrome/ictal state, sleep loss, pharmacologic cognitive slowing

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Brain fog is more informative when linked to timing: orthostatic, post-exertional, migraine/postdrome, medication-timed, or sleep-related.

hypotheses.reason: Orthostatic challenge studies show symptom and cognitive worsening in susceptible groups.

evidence.summary: Real-time and challenge-based studies show brain fog can fluctuate with orthostatic stress, exertion, migraine phase and drug timing.

key_sources.title: Brain Fog Phenotypes and Differential Characterization: A Structured Review
key_sources.relevant_finding: Orthostatic challenge/post-exertion provokes symptom/test worsening in susceptible groups.

limitations: EMA/wearable evidence sparse in many populations

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Brain fog always implies measurable cognitive impairment.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Large cohorts show weak/inconsistent self-report vs objective test relationships.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: Self-reported cognitive cloudiness requiring subtyping by domain,timing and context before mechanism inference.
Brain fog worsened during upright posture or orthostatic challenge.

research_gaps: Validate standardized brain fog tools across non-post-COVID populations.
Link orthostatic/exertional physiological markers with real-time brain fog fluctuations.

most_supported_patterns: Subjective brain fog can occur without objective impairment.
Objective deficits most often affect attention and processing speed.
highest_value_patterns_for_inference: Brain fog phenotype uncertain until timing/context/domain are known.
patterns_requiring_caution: Standard cognitive tests may miss subtle deficits.
recommended_next_queries: Which tests best capture real-world brain fog fluctuations?
How do orthostatic markers map to brain fog severity?
summary.overall_assessment: Brain fog should be modeled as heterogeneous symptom cluster; objective cognition, timing and mechanism context must be separated.

works_cited.title: Brain Fog Phenotypes and Differential Characterization: A Structured Review
